name: Washington Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description: Department of Health, PO Box 47877, Olympia, WA 98504-7877

requiredDocuments.name: Background check
requiredDocuments.description: Washington State Patrol and FBI background check.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Washington SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: $175 initial. $100 annual renewal (Washington uses annual renewal on your birthday).

mainEntity.name: Is Washington part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Washington allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Washington have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Washington participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Washington without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Washington?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
